behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between history-management logic and the LLM request builder. A unit test of trimming alone can miss wiring bugs where the untrimmed list is still sent.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real history-selection module to the real prompt/request-construction module with a fake LLM transport. Feed 15 prior messages, invoke response generation, and assert the outbound LLM payload contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM semantic output quality, which is non-deterministic and not reducible to module wiring checks. Mocking generation would hide the real failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run a fixed evaluation set of realistic support questions against the production model/prompt. Score relevance with predefined rubric criteria and fail if relevance rate drops below threshold, capturing off-topic/generic failures.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior includes tone judgment quality, which depends on model interpretation and can vary across runs. Integration alone can verify branching but not whether the formality detection is actually correct.
behaviors.test_description: Use a labeled set of overly formal candidate replies for casual-tier users and run the real tone-check prompt/model. Assert the checker flags them at/above target recall and that regeneration call path is executed when flagged.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at module boundaries between chat orchestration, regeneration tracking, clock usage, and DB persistence. A pure unit test would miss schema/transaction mapping issues.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the chat flow against a real local test database and fixed clock source. Trigger a case with regeneration and assert all records are written (user, draft, regenerated final) with expected relational links and timestamps.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module state/response-selection guarantee (orchestrator + tone-check result + response presenter). It does not require real model quality if regeneration trigger is forced deterministically.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic test doubles for generator outputs and tone-check result set to trigger regeneration, run end-to-end application logic and assert customer-facing payload contains only second response while rejected draft is retained only in internal audit/log records.
